title: The Supreme Court Judges (Salaries and Conditions of Service) Act, 1958
full_text: The Supreme Court Judges (Salaries and Conditions of Service) Act, 1958 उच्चतम न्यायालय न्यायाधीश (वेतन और सेवा शर्त) अधिनियम, 1958 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 195841 Act Number: 41 Enactment Date: 1958-10-17 Act Year: 1958 Short Title: The Supreme Court Judges (Salaries and Conditions of Service) Act, 1958 Hindi Title: उच्चतम न्यायालय न्यायाधीश (वेतन और सेवा शर्त) अधिनियम, 1958 Long Title: An Act to regulate salaries and certain conditions of service of the Judges of the Supreme Court. Ministry: Ministry of Law and Justice Department: Department of Justice Enforcement Date: 17-10-1958 Schedule 1. THE SCHEDULE Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Supreme Court Judges (Salaries and Conditions of Service) Act, 1958 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II LEAVE CHAPTER III SALARIES AND PENSIONS CHAPTER IV MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title. Show Related Subordinates Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Kinds of leave admissible to a Judge. Section 4. Leave account showing the amount of leave due. Section 4A. Leave encashment. Section 5. Aggregate amount of leave which may be granted. Show Related Subordinates Section 5A. Commutation of leave on half allowances into leave on full allowances. Section 6. Grant of leave not due. Section 7. Special disability leave. Section 8. Extraordinary leave. Section 9. Leave allowances. Section 10. Combining leave with vacation. Section 11. Consequences of over staying leave or vacation. Section 12. Authority competent to grant leave. Section 12A. Salaries of the Judges. Section 13. Pension payable to Judges. Section 13A. Benefit of added years of service. Section 14. Special provisions for pension in respect of Judges who are members of service. Section 15. Power of President to add to the service for pension. Section 16. Extraordinary pension. Section 16A. Family pension and gratuity. Section 16B. Additional quantum of pension or family pension. Section 17. Pension payable to a Judge who was in receipt of pension at the time of appointment as such. Section 18. [Omitted.]. Section 19. Commutation of pension. Section 20. Provident fund. Section 20A. Deposit Linked Insurance Scheme. Section 21. Authority competent to grant pension. Section 22. Travelling allowance to a Judge. Section 23. Facilities for rent-free houses and other conditions of service. Section 23A. Conveyance facilities. Section 23B. Sumptuary allowance. Section 23C. Medical facilities for retired Judges. Section 23D. Exemption from liability to pay Income-tax on certain perquisites received by a Judge. Section 24. Power to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 25. Savings. Schedule 1.
